findings.why_domain_specific: Claim-worthiness was boosted by government order and publication vocabulary.
findings.status: fixed: default substantive detection now uses generic factual, value, date, version, relationship, procedure, and requirement signals.

findings.why_domain_specific: GO numbers are government-order identifiers and caused generic software/product text to share a core value type with government orders.
findings.status: fixed: generic extractor no longer emits GO values by default; government GO extraction is available via extract_government_policy_value_mentions.

findings.why_domain_specific: Default claim taxonomy encoded government/policy labels instead of generic RAG assertion categories.
findings.status: fixed: default labels are value_assertion, date_time_assertion, requirement_or_condition, procedural_assertion, relationship_or_comparison, definition, and general_factual.

findings.exact_term_or_pattern: G.O., circular, notification, policy, scheme, government employees, beneficiaries
findings.why_domain_specific: The rule extractor was explicitly tuned for Indian government/policy text and factory default selected it whenever triplet extraction was enabled.
findings.status: fixed: optional extractor is GovernmentPolicyTripletExtractorV0; default enabled mode is GenericRuleTripletExtractorV0.

findings.exact_term_or_pattern: Preserve exact legal identifiers (G.O. numbers, Act names, dates)
findings.why_domain_specific: LLM prompt instructed extraction to preserve government/legal identifiers as a first-class default requirement.
findings.status: fixed: prompt now preserves generic identifiers, versions, dates, numbers, and names; LLM fallback is generic.

findings.exact_term_or_pattern: government document RAG system; authority; citation: Specific GO number, circular number, order reference
findings.why_domain_specific: Requirement extraction prompt framed every query as government-document analysis.
findings.status: fixed: prompt now uses generic RAG requirement types and maps legacy aliases to generic types.

findings.why_domain_specific: The schema used policy/legal-style requirement categories as the primary type system.
findings.status: fixed: generic requirement types were added; legacy aliases remain accepted for compatibility.

findings.why_domain_specific: A geographic-scope heuristic recognizes US state names, which is domain metadata rather than a universal RAG concept.
findings.status: partially fixed: added generic scoped qualifier terms; full location/entity scope extraction remains future work.

findings.why_domain_specific: Department is common in government corpora, but can be generic organizational metadata.
findings.status: kept: not used as a decision signal.

findings.exact_term_or_pattern: missing publication_date, updated_at, version, current_version support
findings.why_domain_specific: Relying mainly on issue_date/timestamp under-covered software, product, finance, and scientific source metadata.
findings.status: fixed: publication_date, updated_at, version, current_version, current, stale, and draft status handling added.

findings.why_domain_specific: Ambiguity detection is tuned to return/policy-like queries.
findings.status: open: not modified in this pass because it is confidence-sidecar logic, not core grounding/sufficiency/version validity.

findings.why_domain_specific: Eligibility wording is policy-flavored, though the underlying failure is generic overgeneralization beyond an explicit only constraint.
findings.status: open: rationale should be renamed to generic scope-overgeneralization in a follow-up.

findings.exact_term_or_pattern: Andhra Pradesh G.O. order PDFs, DEPARTMENT, ORDER, NOTIFICATION
AP, Andhra Pradesh, G.O.Ms, Rule, District
findings.status: kept; supplemented by domain_agnostic tests and benchmark.
